name: Racial or Ethnic Origin
description: Race, ethnicity, national origin, or tribal affiliation. Includes both self-identified and inferred racial/ethnic data.

regulatory_basis.name: GDPR Article 9(1)
regulatory_basis.summary: Racial or ethnic origin is a special category of personal data.

regulatory_basis.name: US Fair Housing Act
regulatory_basis.summary: Prohibits targeting by race, color, or national origin in housing advertising.

regulatory_basis.name: US ECOA
regulatory_basis.summary: Prohibits targeting by race, color, or national origin in credit advertising.

regulatory_basis.name: US Title VII / EEOC
regulatory_basis.summary: Prohibits targeting by race, color, or national origin in employment advertising.

includes: Language-as-proxy when used to infer ethnicity
Multicultural audience segments that define audiences by racial/ethnic identity
excludes: Language preference for ad delivery (serving ads in Spanish is not ethnic targeting)
Country of residence (geo-targeting by country is not ethnic origin targeting)
Cultural interest segments (e.g., 'interested in Korean cuisine') unless used as ethnic proxy
guidance: The proxy problem is central here. Zip code targeting in the US can function as a racial proxy (redlining). Governance agents evaluating housing/employment/credit campaigns should consider geo-targeting specificity alongside explicit racial/ethnic signals. Language targeting is generally acceptable for ad delivery but not when used as an ethnic proxy for exclusion.
